meta.title: GLSL Shader Optimization Techniques for SOTY Event Horizon
meta.author: Cleanlystudio
meta.description: Comprehensive shader optimization database for high-performance fragment-heavy rendering

alu_optimization.description: Arithmetic Logic Unit optimizations — reducing the computational cost of shader math

techniques.name: Prefer MAD (multiply-add) operations
techniques.description: a * b + c is a single instruction (FMA/MAD) on most GPUs. Restructure math to use this pattern.
techniques.before: float result = a * b; result = result + c;

techniques.name: Use swizzling instead of constructors
techniques.description: Accessing .xyz is free — constructing a new vec3 may not be

techniques.name: Replace pow(x, 2.0) with x*x
techniques.description: pow() is a transcendental function (expensive). For small integer exponents, multiply is faster.

pow_4.after: float x2 = x*x; float result = x2*x2;

techniques.name: Use step() instead of if/else
techniques.description: step(edge, x) returns 0.0 if x < edge, else 1.0. Branchless on all GPUs.
techniques.before: float result; if (x > threshold) { result = a; } else { result = b; }
techniques.after: float s = step(threshold, x); float result = mix(b, a, s);
techniques.savings: Eliminates dynamic branching (see flow control section)

techniques.name: smoothstep() is practically free
techniques.description: smoothstep(a, b, x) computes a smooth Hermite interpolation. On most GPUs this maps to 1-2 instructions.
techniques.usage: Use for soft transitions, edge anti-aliasing, distance-based falloffs

techniques.name: Avoid division — multiply by reciprocal
techniques.description: Division is 4-8x slower than multiplication on most GPUs
techniques.after: float invB = 1.0 / b; float result = a * invB;
techniques.when_beneficial: When dividing by the same value multiple times. For single use, compiler usually optimizes.
techniques.note: Pre-compute reciprocals on CPU when possible and pass as uniforms

techniques.name: Avoid sqrt when comparing distances
techniques.description: length() uses sqrt internally. For comparisons, squared distance is sufficient.
techniques.after: vec3 diff = a - b; if (dot(diff, diff) < radius * radius) { ... }

techniques.name: Use built-in functions over manual implementations
techniques.description: GPU built-ins (dot, cross, normalize, reflect, refract, clamp, mix, fract) are hardware-accelerated
techniques.rule: NEVER implement your own version of a GLSL built-in function. The hardware version is always faster.

examples.dot_product: dot(a, b) — hardware 1 cycle vs manual a.x*b.x + a.y*b.y + a.z*b.z (3+ cycles)
examples.normalize: normalize(v) — hardware accelerated vs v / length(v) (division + sqrt)
examples.clamp: clamp(x, 0.0, 1.0) — hardware vs max(0.0, min(x, 1.0)) (2 instructions)

techniques.name: Minimize transcendental functions
techniques.description: sin, cos, tan, exp, log, pow, sqrt are expensive (4-16 cycles). Use sparingly.
alternatives: Pre-compute sin/cos values in a texture lookup
Use polynomial approximations for rough estimates
Compute on CPU and pass as uniforms when value doesn't change per-pixel

dependent_texture_reads.description: A dependent texture read is when the UV coordinate used for a texture fetch is computed FROM another texture read. Very expensive.
dependent_texture_reads.example_bad: vec2 uv = texture(noiseMap, coord).rg; vec4 color = texture(colorMap, uv);
dependent_texture_reads.problem: GPU cannot pre-fetch the second texture because it doesn't know the address until the first read completes. Stalls the pipeline.
solutions: Pre-compute UV offsets on CPU when possible
Use the vertex shader to compute UVs and pass via varying (vertex shader is rarely the bottleneck)
Combine textures to reduce dependency chains
If unavoidable, minimize the number of dependent reads in the chain

texture_lod.description: textureLod() explicitly specifies the mipmap level, avoiding automatic LOD calculation
texture_lod.when_to_use: When you know the exact LOD needed (e.g., GPGPU reads from data textures, screen-space effects at known resolution)
texture_lod.benefit: Eliminates derivative calculation for LOD and allows more aggressive pre-fetching
texture_lod.example: vec4 data = textureLod(dataTexture, uv, 0.0); // Always sample mip level 0

cache_texture_reads.description: If you need the same texture value multiple times, read it once into a local variable
cache_texture_reads.before: float r = texture(tex, uv).r; float g = texture(tex, uv).g;
cache_texture_reads.after: vec4 sample = texture(tex, uv); float r = sample.r; float g = sample.g;
cache_texture_reads.note: The compiler may optimize this, but don't rely on it. Be explicit.

texture_cache_locality.description: GPUs have texture caches. Adjacent pixels reading from adjacent UV coordinates benefit from cache hits.
implications: Sequential UV patterns (linear gradients) are cache-friendly
Random UV patterns (noise-based lookups) cause cache thrashing
Smaller textures fit better in cache — don't use 4096×4096 when 1024×1024 suffices

dynamic_branching.description: if/else in fragment shaders causes GPU inefficiency because fragments in the same warp/wavefront may take different paths
dynamic_branching.mechanism: GPUs process fragments in groups (warps: 32 on NVIDIA, wavefronts: 64 on AMD). If any fragment in the group takes a branch, ALL fragments execute BOTH branches and discard the unused result.
dynamic_branching.worst_case: If 1 of 32 fragments takes the 'if' path and 31 take the 'else', all 32 execute both paths — 2x the work.
rules: Avoid dynamic branching in fragment shaders when possible
Branches on uniform values are free (all fragments take the same path)
Branches on varying/attribute values may cause divergence
Branches on texture-derived values are the worst (unpredictable)

compile_time_branching.description: Use #ifdef / #if for quality levels instead of runtime if statements

example.bad: if (u_quality > 2) { /* expensive raymarching */ } else { /* cheap approximation */ }

compile_time_branching.implementation: Compile different shader variants for each quality level. Switch programs based on device capability.
compile_time_branching.note: This means maintaining multiple shader versions, but the performance gain is significant

step_for_if.description: Replace if (x > threshold) { result = a; } else { result = b; }
step_for_if.code: float s = step(threshold, x); float result = mix(b, a, s);

sign_for_direction.description: Replace if (x > 0.0) return 1.0; else if (x < 0.0) return -1.0; else return 0.0;

unroll_small_loops.description: For loops with small, known iteration counts, manual unrolling avoids loop overhead
unroll_small_loops.threshold: Unroll loops with < 4 iterations. Let compiler handle 4-16. Avoid > 64 iterations in fragment shader.

example.before: for (int i = 0; i < 3; i++) { result += weights[i] * texture(tex, uv + offsets[i]).rgb; }

early_termination.description: Break out of loops early when the result is determined
early_termination.caveat: Only beneficial if MOST fragments terminate early. If only a few do, it doesn't help (warp divergence).

precision.description: GLSL precision qualifiers control floating-point precision and performance

highp.use: Position calculations, world-space coordinates, time (accumulated values), ray origin/direction

mediump.use: Colors, UV coordinates, normals, most intermediate calculations
mediump.performance: Up to 2x faster than highp on mobile (no difference on desktop)

lowp.use: Final color output, alpha, simple multipliers
lowp.performance: Fastest on mobile (marginal vs mediump on most modern mobile GPUs)

platform_differences.desktop: All qualifiers map to 32-bit float. Precision qualifiers are essentially ignored. No performance difference.
platform_differences.mobile: Precision qualifiers are respected. mediump is significantly faster than highp on Adreno/Mali/PowerVR.
platform_differences.apple_mobile: Apple GPUs (A-series, M-series) handle precision well. mediump is faster but highp is not dramatically slow.

strategy.default_fragment_precision: precision mediump float; — default to mediump in fragment shaders
strategy.explicit_highp: Use highp explicitly for: accumulated time, world positions, raymarching steps, anything that accumulates error
strategy.explicit_lowp: Use lowp for: final fragColor output, simple alpha calculations

common_precision_bugs.cause: mediump doesn't have enough precision for subtle gradient transitions
common_precision_bugs.fix: Use highp for the gradient calculation, or add dithering noise

common_precision_bugs.cause: World-space positions too large for mediump (values > 65504 overflow)
common_precision_bugs.fix: Use highp for position calculations, or work in view-space (smaller values)

common_precision_bugs.bug: Time-based animations break after ~60 seconds on mobile
common_precision_bugs.cause: u_time accumulates beyond mediump precision. At 60fps, time = 60.0 has only 0.03 precision.
common_precision_bugs.fix: Use highp for time, or use fract(time * frequency) to keep values small

raymarching_optimization.description: Raymarching (sphere tracing) is the core technique for rendering the black hole. Optimization is critical for 60fps.
raymarching_optimization.basic_algorithm: For each pixel: cast ray from camera. Step along ray, evaluating SDF. When distance < threshold, record hit.

variable_step_size.description: Step by the SDF distance (sphere tracing). Near surfaces, steps are tiny. Far from surfaces, steps are huge.
variable_step_size.implementation: float d = sdf(p); p += rayDir * d; // step exactly by the distance to nearest surface
variable_step_size.advantage: Converges to surface in O(log n) steps instead of O(n)

early_termination.description: Stop marching when accumulated opacity exceeds threshold or ray has traveled too far
early_termination.savings: Avoids wasting cycles on fully opaque or empty regions

bounding_volume_pretest.description: Before raymarching, test if the ray intersects a simple bounding shape (sphere, box)
bounding_volume_pretest.implementation: Analytically test ray-sphere intersection. If miss, skip raymarching entirely.
bounding_volume_pretest.savings: Pixels not pointing at the black hole skip the expensive loop entirely (~50-80% of pixels in many frames)

distance_field_acceleration.description: Use a pre-computed 3D distance field texture for complex shapes
distance_field_acceleration.implementation: Sample 3D texture instead of computing SDF analytically. Requires 3D texture support (WebGL 2.0).

adaptive_step_count.description: Reduce MAX_STEPS on mobile or lower quality tiers
adaptive_step_count.implementation: #define MAX_STEPS 128 // change via shader variant per quality tier

depth_buffer_rejection.description: Use the depth buffer from the rasterized scene to skip raymarching behind opaque geometry
depth_buffer_rejection.implementation: Sample depth texture at fragment UV. Convert to world distance. If ray start distance > depth, discard.
depth_buffer_rejection.savings: Eliminates raymarching for pixels fully occluded by rasterized geometry

temporal_reprojection.description: Reuse previous frame's raymarching results, only re-march pixels that changed significantly
temporal_reprojection.implementation: Store previous frame's result in a texture. Reproject to current frame using motion vectors. Only re-march if reprojection error exceeds threshold.
temporal_reprojection.savings: 50-80% reduction in per-frame raymarching cost for slow-moving scenes
temporal_reprojection.caveat: Introduces ghosting artifacts if not properly handled. Requires motion vector computation.

geodesic_raytracing.description: Light bends around black holes following geodesics in curved spacetime (Schwarzschild metric)
geodesic_raytracing.implementation: Instead of straight-line raymarching, integrate the geodesic equation: d²x/dλ² + Γ dx/dλ dx/dλ = 0
geodesic_raytracing.simplification: For visual accuracy without full GR: deflect rays using an angular deflection formula based on impact parameter
geodesic_raytracing.formula: δ ≈ 4GM / (c²b) for weak field (far from black hole). For strong field, use numerical integration.
geodesic_raytracing.optimization: Pre-compute deflection table as a 1D texture indexed by impact parameter. Lookup instead of computing.

accretion_disk_rendering.description: The disk is a thin torus of hot material. Render it as a flat ring with volumetric scattering.
optimization: Analytical ray-plane intersection for the disk midplane — no raymarching needed for the basic hit test
Add volumetric thickness with just 2-4 additional samples above/below the midplane
Color from temperature profile: T ∝ r^(-3/4) from the Shakura-Sunyaev model

kawase_blur.description: Iterative dual-filter blur — cheaper than Gaussian for same visual quality at large radii
kawase_blur.algorithm: Downsample with 2×2 filter → blur at each mip level → upsample and accumulate
implementation_steps: 2. Downsample to 1/2 res with bilinear 4-tap filter
5. Composite with original at full res

half_resolution.description: Bloom at half resolution is visually indistinguishable from full resolution because bloom is inherently blurry
half_resolution.savings: 4x fewer fragment shader invocations for the bloom pass
half_resolution.implementation: Render bright-pass to half-res FBO. Blur at half res. Upsample and composite.

bilateral_upsample.description: When upsampling half-res effects, use depth-aware bilateral filter to prevent bleeding across depth edges
bilateral_upsample.implementation: Compare depth at each sample point. Weight contribution by depth similarity.
bilateral_upsample.benefit: Sharp edges preserved despite half-res processing

chromatic_aberration.description: Separate RGB channels with slight offset — simulates lens imperfection and gravitational lensing
chromatic_aberration.cheap_version: Sample texture 3 times with slightly offset UVs for R, G, B
chromatic_aberration.expensive_version: Radial chromatic aberration — offset increases with distance from center (physically accurate for lens distortion)
chromatic_aberration.cost: 3 texture reads instead of 1 — reasonable. Don't use more than 3 samples.
chromatic_aberration.event_horizon_use: Chromatic aberration increases near the black hole edge — simulates extreme spacetime curvature splitting wavelengths

tone_mapping.description: Map HDR render values to displayable LDR range

operators.name: Reinhard
operators.character: Gentle rolloff. Classic. Can look washed out.

operators.name: ACES
operators.character: Film-like. Rich shadows, controlled highlights. Industry standard.

operators.name: Uncharted 2
operators.character: Similar to ACES. Slightly different shoulder.

operators.name: AgX
operators.character: Better color preservation in highlights. Modern best practice.

tone_mapping.recommendation: ACES for cinematic feel. AgX for more accurate color preservation. Both are single-pass and trivially cheap.

vignette.description: Darken edges of the screen — focuses attention on center (the black hole)
vignette.cost: Essentially free — 1 smoothstep operation per pixel
vignette.event_horizon_use: Strong vignette draws focus to the central black hole. Vary intensity with scroll position.

film_grain.description: Subtle noise overlay simulating photographic film grain — adds organic feel and breaks banding
film_grain.intensity: 0.02-0.05 (2-5% opacity) — subtle enough to not be consciously noticed but breaks gradient banding
film_grain.cost: Essentially free — 1 sin, 1 fract per pixel
film_grain.event_horizon_use: Critical for preventing banding on dark gradients (space backgrounds). Apply after tone mapping.

ext_disjoint_timer_query.description: WebGL 2.0 extension for measuring GPU time of operations
ext_disjoint_timer_query.note: Results are in nanoseconds. Check for disjoint with gl.getParameter(ext.GPU_DISJOINT_EXT).
ext_disjoint_timer_query.availability: May be disabled in some browsers (Firefox hides it by default, Chrome enables it)

spector_js.description: Capture a complete WebGL frame — all draw calls, state changes, textures, buffers
spector_js.usage: Install as browser extension. Click capture button. Inspect the full frame breakdown.
what_to_look_for: Redundant state changes (same texture bound multiple times)
Large textures that could be smaller

performance_panel.description: Chrome DevTools Performance panel for frame-level analysis
performance_panel.what_to_record: Start recording → interact with the experience → stop recording
what_to_look_for: Long JavaScript tasks blocking the main thread
GPU time (in the GPU lane of the timeline)

chunk_system.description: Break shaders into reusable chunks/includes to avoid code duplication
chunk_system.technique: Use string concatenation or template literals to compose shaders from chunks

chunks.name: common_uniforms
chunks.content: Shared uniforms: time, resolution, mouse, scrollProgress

chunks.name: sdf_primitives
chunks.content: Sphere, box, torus, cylinder, capsule SDF functions

chunks.name: black_hole
chunks.content: Schwarzschild metric, geodesic ray bending, accretion disk

quality_variants.description: Compile different shader variants for different quality tiers using #define
quality_variants.implementation: Prepend #define statements to shader source before compilation based on detected quality tier
